briefdescription.para: Dequantize a single Q5_0 block to FP32.

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *A, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q5_0

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *A_q8, const void *B_q5, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q5_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Batch GEMM with Q5_0 weights and Q8_0 activations for prefill.

detaileddescription.para: Computes C = A @ B^T + bias where: A: [M x K] Q8_0 quantized activations (M tokens, K features) B: [N x K] Q5_0 quantized weights (N outputs, K features) C: [M x N] FP32 output
This is the INT8 batch kernel for prefill, using pre-quantized activations to avoid FP32->Q8_0 conversion overhead per operation.

parameterdescription.para: Input activations in Q8_0 format [M rows of K/32 blocks each]
Weights in Q5_0 format [N rows of K/32 blocks each]
Optional bias vector [N], NULL if not used
Output matrix [M x N], row-major FP32

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *A, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q5_0_ref

briefdescription.para: GEMM with transposed Q5_0 weights: C = A @ B^T.

parameterdescription.para: Input activations [M x K], row-major FP32
Weight matrix in Q5_0 format [N x K], row-major quantized
Optional bias [N], NULL if not used
Output dimension (number of rows in B)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *A, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q5_0_sse_v2

memberdef.argsstring: (float *Y, const void *W, const float *X, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_q5_0

memberdef.argsstring: (float *dX, const void *W, const float *dY, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_q5_0_backward

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const float *x, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q5_0

briefdescription.para: Auto-dispatch GEMV for Q5_0 weights based on CPU features.

detaileddescription.para:
Dispatch priority (best available):
AVX-512 (512-bit vectors) - Intel Skylake-X+
AVX2+FMA (256-bit vectors) - Intel Haswell+
AVX (256-bit vectors) - Intel Sandy Bridge+
SSE4.1 (128-bit vectors) - Intel Nehalem+
Reference (scalar) - Fallback
Uses ck_features.h for standardized feature detection.

parameterdescription.para: Weight matrix in Q5_0 format [M x K]

memberdef.argsstring: (float *dX, const void *W, const float *dY, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q5_0_backward

memberdef.argsstring: (float *dX, const void *W, const float *dY, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q5_0_backward_ref

parameterdescription.para: Weight matrix in Q5_0 format [M x K]

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const float *x, int M, int K, int ith, int nth)
memberdef.name: gemv_q5_0_parallel

briefdescription.para: Parallel reference GEMV for Q5_0 × FP32.

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const float *x, int M, int K, int ith, int nth)
memberdef.name: gemv_q5_0_parallel_simd

briefdescription.para: Parallel SIMD GEMV for Q5_0 × FP32 with prefetching.

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const void *x_q8, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q5_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Matrix-vector multiply with Q5_0 weights and Q8_0 input.

parameterdescription.para: Weight matrix in Q5_0 format [M x K]
Number of columns (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const float *x, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q5_0_ref

briefdescription.para: Matrix-vector multiply with Q5_0 weights (scalar reference)

parameterdescription.para: Weight matrix in Q5_0 format [M x K]
Number of columns (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (int n, float *s, const void *vx, const void *vy)
memberdef.name: vec_dot_q5_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Auto-dispatch quantized dot product Q5_0 x Q8_0.

detaileddescription.para:
Dispatch priority:
AVX512 (best performance on modern Intel/AMD)
AVX (256-bit float ops, works on Sandy/Ivy Bridge and newer)
SSSE3 (128-bit fallback)
Reference scalar (last resort)

memberdef.argsstring: (int n, float *s, const void *vx, const void *vy)
memberdef.name: vec_dot_q5_0_q8_0_ref

briefdescription.para: Quantized dot product: Q5_0 weights x Q8_0 input (scalar reference)

parameterdescription.para: Number of elements (must be multiple of 32)

briefdescription.para: GEMM/GEMV kernels with Q5_0 quantized weights.

sect1.title: CK-ENGINE KERNEL RULES:

listitem.para: NO malloc/free - memory via bump allocator, pointers passed in
NO OpenMP - parallelization at orchestrator/codegen layer
API must define: inputs, outputs, workspace, and memory layouts
Pure computation - deterministic, no side effects

sect1.para: After changes: make test && make llamacpp-parity-full

Q5_0 Format:
32 weights per block
1 FP16 scale per block
Low 4-bits stored like Q4_0 (16 bytes)
High 1-bit packed separately (4 bytes)
22 bytes per 32 weights = 5.5 bits/weight
Dequantization: w = scale * (q5 - 16) where q5 = low4bit | (highbit << 4), giving values 0-31, then subtract 16 for signed -16 to +15
Operations: Forward: Y = W @ X (W is Q5_0, X and Y are FP32) Backward: dX = W^T @ dY (gradient w.r.t. input)
